skill.name: workflow-interactive-dev
skill.description: Guide for developing interactive response nodes in FastGPT workflows, including backend dispatch, frontend rendering, state recovery, and localization changes.
skill.summary: Develop FastGPT workflow interactive response nodes with coordinated backend, frontend, and history handling steps.
skill.author: labring

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are documentation examples inside SKILL.md rather than executable code. The relative import paths, property names such as key, and TypeScript snippets are false positives; the remaining risk is low because the skill guides broad source edits across a FastGPT repository.

low_findings.title: Broad Repository File Modification Guidance
low_findings.description: The skill instructs users to modify many backend, frontend, runtime, and localization files in a FastGPT repository. This is expected for the development task, but users should review changes carefully because mistakes can affect workflow execution and chat history behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The listed file paths show clear filesystem modification guidance. The intent is legitimate development documentation, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the reviewed locations are Markdown inline code, file paths, or TypeScript code fences. No executable shell command or command injection path was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious backticks are Markdown formatting around paths, identifiers, and code examples. There is no shell script, Ruby code, or runtime command execution in the skill file.

low_findings.title: Static Path Traversal Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The path traversal alerts come from TypeScript relative import examples within code fences. They do not read arbitrary user-controlled files or escape a runtime sandbox.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are import statements in documentation examples, including standard relative module imports. No filesystem API or user-controlled path construction is present.

low_findings.title: Static Sensitive Key Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The certificate or key alerts correspond to object property usage such as input.key in frontend form examples. No private key, credential, certificate material, or secret value is embedded.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines use key as an object property and React key value. This is a common UI pattern and not evidence of secret material.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts appear to be keyword collisions in names, descriptions, or TypeScript examples. No hashing, encryption, certificate validation, or cryptographic algorithm use was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found workflow and UI guidance at these locations, not crypto code. The scanner likely matched short tokens inside ordinary text or identifiers.

content.value_statement: FastGPT interactive workflow nodes require coordinated changes across shared types, dispatch logic, UI components, and saved history. This skill gives a practical implementation checklist for adding a new interactive response safely.
actual_capabilities: Explains the supported FastGPT interactive node types and their two-stage execution model.
Identifies backend type, enum, template, dispatch, and callback files that need updates.
Shows how to add frontend chat and workflow editor components for a custom interactive node.
Covers isEntry state handling, skip node queues, tool responses, and history rewriting.
Lists localization files and testing scenarios required for a complete implementation.
limitations: It is a written development guide and does not execute changes automatically.
Examples use placeholder node names that must be adapted to the real feature.
It assumes familiarity with the FastGPT repository structure and TypeScript codebase.
It does not validate business rules or user input schemas for a specific custom node.

use_cases.title: Add a Custom Form Interaction
use_cases.description: Use the guide to define a new interactive node that pauses a workflow, collects structured user input, and resumes execution.

use_cases.title: Review Workflow Runtime Changes
use_cases.description: Check dispatch, callback registration, isEntry handling, and history rewriting before approving an interactive workflow change.

use_cases.title: Implement Matching UI Components
use_cases.description: Create chat and workflow editor components that render node parameters and submit user responses back to the runtime.

prompt_templates.title: Map Required Files
prompt_templates.prompt: Using this skill, list the FastGPT files I need to edit for a new interactive workflow node named approvalCheck.

prompt_templates.title: Create Backend Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan the backend type, template, dispatch, callback, and isEntry changes for a FastGPT interactive node.

prompt_templates.title: Design UI Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design the chat component and workflow editor component for a custom FastGPT interactive node.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my FastGPT interactive node implementation against this skill and identify missing runtime, history, localization, or test coverage.

output_examples.input: I need to add an approval step to a FastGPT workflow.
output: A file-by-file implementation checklist for shared types, node template, dispatch logic, callback registration, UI components, and translations.
Notes about the first execution returning an interactive response and the second execution consuming the submitted value.
A focused test list for rendering, persistence, resume behavior, history updates, and localization.

output_examples.input: My interactive node resumes incorrectly after user input.
output: A review path for isEntry handling, lastInteractive matching, skipNodeQueue behavior, and rewritten chat histories.
Likely files to inspect in dispatch, runtime utilities, chat save logic, and chat history helpers.

output_examples.input: I want to review a pull request that adds a workflow interaction.
output: A reviewer checklist covering backend registration, frontend rendering, data parsing, output mapping, translations, and regression tests.
Risk areas to verify before merge, including history mutation and tool response compatibility.

best_practices: Start with the shared interactive response type before editing runtime or UI code.
Keep the two execution phases explicit: request interaction first, then process submitted input.
Test resume behavior, saved history, translations, and downstream node outputs together.
anti_patterns: Adding a UI component without registering matching backend dispatch behavior.
Forgetting to reset isEntry after the submitted interaction is processed.
Parsing user input without defining the expected shape for the custom node.

faq.question: Does this skill create the node automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation guidance and examples for developers working in the FastGPT codebase.

faq.question: Which parts of FastGPT does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers shared workflow types, node templates, dispatch logic, frontend components, history utilities, and localization files.

faq.question: Can it help debug a node that runs twice?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains that interactive nodes have a first phase for pausing and a second phase for processing user input.

faq.question: Does it include production-ready code?
faq.answer: No. The snippets are templates with placeholder names and must be adapted, reviewed, and tested.

faq.question: What should I test after using it?
faq.answer: Test editor rendering, saved configuration, chat rendering, submitted values, workflow resume behavior, history updates, and translations.

faq.question: Is it specific to Claude, Codex, or Claude Code?
faq.answer: It can guide Claude, Codex, or Claude Code when they help modify a FastGPT repository.
